phase_full_name: Phase 31CO — Metadata-Only Planned Manifest Consumer / Loader Preflight Planner
scope: metadata-only. No model load. No Q2_K/SDIR blob generation. No raw activation generation. No runtime loader implementation. No runtime integration. No llama.cpp modification or rebuild. No loader source code. No compiled binary.
source_inputs_reviewed: SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md (post-31CN-hotfix; current allowed next phase is Phase 31CO planning-only)
docs/PHASE31CN_PLANNED_MANIFEST_NORMALIZER_PROVENANCE_ADAPTER.md (350 lines; the 31CN derivative-extension adapter pattern + 5 pipeline steps + 15 self-tests)

src/results/PHASE31CN_NORMALIZED_PLANNED_MANIFEST.json (621 lines, 22.5 KB; the canonical 31CN-normalized planned manifest with all 11 derivative fields + 9 layer-family entries + 7 legacy-sidecar globs + 9 runtime safety invariants + 28 required top-level fields)

src/results/PHASE31CN_PLANNED_MANIFEST_NORMALIZER_RESULT.json (251 lines, 8.9 KB; 18/18 round-trip consistency checks, 15/15 self-tests, 50/50 31CI validator rules, 0/0 silent bypass / filtered provenance rules)
src/results/PHASE31CM_DRY_RUN_OUTPUT_VALIDATION.json (546 lines, 17 KB; 12/12 round-trip checks pass, 13/13 self-tests pass, 0 forbidden findings, 7 R-provenance rules documented)
docs/PHASE31CL_METADATA_ONLY_ARTIFACT_WRITER_DRY_RUN.md (505 lines; the 16 R_DRY_* fail-safe rules + 31CI validator integration at pre-plan and post-plan)
src/results/PHASE31CL_ARTIFACT_WRITER_DRY_RUN_OUTPUT.json (18 KB; 17 planned files, 3 layers x 3 families, ffn_down no SDIR, byte accounting)

docs/PHASE31CH_RUNTIME_ARTIFACT_FORMAT_LOADER_PLANNING.md (705 lines; the v1.1.0 manifest schema + 9 runtime safety invariants + 7 legacy-sidecar exclusion globs + 48 loader validation rules R1-R48 + 4 loader architecture options)
docs/SDI_OPERATOR_SKILL_PACK_PLAN.md (the 8-skill operator pack plan; 31CO was tested via sdi_phase_gate_check, sdi_preflight_check, sdi_sot_drift_scan, sdi_next_phase_prompt_builder)

preflight_check_list.pass_criteria: file exists, file is valid UTF-8, JSON parses, top-level is a dict
preflight_check_list.fail_fast_trigger: file not found; permission denied; JSON parse error; top-level not a dict

preflight_check_list.action: iterate the 28 required top-level fields and verify each is present with the expected type
preflight_check_list.pass_criteria: all 28 fields present, all have the expected type (bool, str, int, list, dict)
preflight_check_list.fail_fast_trigger: any required field missing or wrong type
preflight_check_list.no_go_reason: if metadata_only != true OR dry_run != true OR write_binary != false, the manifest is not a valid metadata-only dry-run plan; escalate to operator

preflight_check_list.pass_criteria: all 3 fields present with the expected values
preflight_check_list.fail_fast_trigger: any of the 3 fields missing or wrong value
preflight_check_list.no_go_reason: if created_by_phase != '31CI', the manifest is a derivative that is not using the 31CN adapter pattern; the 31CI validator would hard-fail it; a derivative that claims to be a 31CI-original is a provenance violation; escalate to operator

preflight_check_list.action: verify the 11 explicit derivative provenance fields are all present (normalized_from_phase, normalized_from_artifact, normalized_from_artifact_sha256, source_manifest_phase, source_manifest_artifact, source_manifest_artifact_sha256, validator_phase, validator_artifact, validator_artifact_sha256, normalized_by_phase, normalized_at_utc)

preflight_check_list.pass_criteria: validation_result.passed == true, error_count == 0, warning_count == 0 (or 0 in strict mode), rules_checked_count == 50
preflight_check_list.fail_fast_trigger: any error; any warning in strict mode; any rule not run (e.g. validator short-circuited)
preflight_check_list.no_go_reason: if any of the 7 R-provenance rules fail, the derivative-extension adapter pattern was bypassed or corrupted; escalate to operator

preflight_check_list.action: call src.phase31cm_dry_run_output_validator on the 31CN-normalized manifest (as if it were a 31CL output)
preflight_check_list.fail_fast_trigger: any of the 12 checks fail; any forbidden finding; provenance filters not documented
preflight_check_list.no_go_reason: if byte_accounting fails (planned total bytes don't match expected), the manifest is internally inconsistent and a loader would not know how much memory to allocate; escalate to operator

preflight_check_list.action: scan the manifest for any key matching the 7 31CH legacy-sidecar exclusion glob patterns (prt_*, sidecar_*, g_prt_*, g_build_ffn_*, g_apply_layer_*, shadow_*, pager_*); verify the 9 31CH runtime safety invariants are all true
preflight_check_list.pass_criteria: no key matches any of the 7 globs; all 9 runtime safety invariants present and true
preflight_check_list.fail_fast_trigger: any key matches a legacy-sidecar exclusion glob; any runtime safety invariant missing or false
preflight_check_list.no_go_reason: if any legacy-sidecar glob matches, the dormant Phase 11BB / 24P / 28BR-AT sidecar machinery is being reactivated, which caused the 31CF BLOCKED classification; escalate to operator

preflight_check_list.action: evaluate the 6 loader-readiness checks (see loader_readiness_boundary section)
preflight_check_list.no_go_reason: if any of the 4 no-go conditions (no_legacy_sidecar_reactivation, no_real_sha256_in_placeholder, no_silent_provenance_bypass, no_derivative_lies) evaluates to TRUE, escalate to operator

fail_fast_rule_list.fail_fast_trigger: any other value at the checkpoint
any other value at the checkpoint
any other value at the checkpoint

fail_fast_rule_list.checks: loaded tensor actual bytes == planned byte count
loaded tensor family in {ffn_up, ffn_gate, ffn_down}
loaded tensor layer index in unique_layer_indices
no key in the loaded tensor matches a legacy-sidecar exclusion glob
fail_fast_rule_list.no_go_reason: legacy-sidecar reactivation; escalate to operator (31CF BLOCKED scenario)
real SHA-256 means a real file was written; out of scope for 31CO; escalate to operator
real SHA-256 means a real file was written; out of scope for 31CO; escalate to operator
false value means a build_ffn patch was applied, which is the 31CF BLOCKED scenario; escalate to operator
false value means the legacy PRT/SDI sidecar is being reactivated; escalate to operator

consumer_contract_summary.consumer_purpose: a future metadata-only Python module (NOT in 31CO; reserved for a future 31CP-style phase) that would (1) read the 31CN-normalized planned manifest JSON, (2) run the 8-step preflight sequence, (3) evaluate the 6 loader-readiness checks, (4) report PASS / FAIL / BLOCKED with explicit fail-fast triggers and no-go conditions
consumer_inputs: the 31CN-normalized planned manifest JSON (the canonical input)
a path to a future 31CO-implemented consumer module (NOT in 31CO; reserved for 31CP)
a path to the 31CI validator module
a path to the 31CM round-trip validator module
a --escalate-cmd flag (optional; default print to stderr)
consumer_does_NOT_take_as_inputs: a model file path (no model load)
a GGUF file path (no GGUF inspection)
a Q2_K blob path (no Q2_K loading)
a SDIR blob path (no SDIR loading)
a raw activation path (no raw activation loading)
consumer_outputs: a preflight report JSON (8-step preflight results + 6 loader-readiness check results, each with passed/message/details)
a loader-readiness verdict (PASS_LOADER_READINESS, BLOCKED_LOADER_READINESS, or BLOCKED_LOADER_READINESS_ESCALATE)
a no-go findings list (if any no-go conditions are TRUE; each with condition_id, triggered_by, operator_action_required=true)
consumer_does_NOT_produce: a compiled consumer module (no compile in 31CO)

checks.name: manifest_is_metadata_only_dryrun_no_binary_write
checks.pass_criteria: all 3 booleans have the expected values
checks.fail_semantics: manifest is writer-ready, not reader-ready; not loader-ready

checks.name: ci_validator_50_rules_0_errors_0_warnings_no_filtering
checks.fail_semantics: any rule short-circuited, any error, any warning, any silent bypass, any provenance rule filter -> not loader-ready

checks.name: cm_roundtrip_12_of_12_zero_forbidden_findings
checks.fail_semantics: any check fails, any forbidden finding, provenance filters not documented -> not loader-ready

checks.name: legacy_sidecar_zero_matches_and_9_runtime_safety_invariants_true
inputs: 0 keys match any of the 7 legacy-sidecar exclusion globs
checks.fail_semantics: any glob match or any invariant false -> not loader-ready AND escalate (no-go)

checks.name: byte_accounting_matches_ci_sample_manifest
checks.pass_criteria: all byte values match expected (exact equality)
checks.fail_semantics: any byte value off by even 1 -> not loader-ready (loader would allocate wrong memory size)

checks.name: all_15_sha256_placeholders_metadata_only_placeholder
inputs: 6 sdir_sha256 placeholders (one per ffn_up/ffn_gate entry across 3 layers)
checks.pass_criteria: all 15 placeholders present with the expected value
checks.fail_semantics: any placeholder missing or not 'metadata_only_placeholder' -> not loader-ready (manifest claims a real file was written; that is out of scope for 31CO)

no_go_conditions.name: manifest_is_not_metadata_only
no_go_conditions.trigger: manifest.metadata_only != true OR manifest.dry_run != true OR manifest.write_binary != false
no_go_conditions.action: refuse to start, escalate to operator
no_go_conditions.why: a manifest that is not metadata-only dry-run no-binary-write is a writer manifest, not a reader manifest; a consumer would not be the right tool; an operator should review whether the manifest is correctly classified

no_go_conditions.name: legacy_sidecar_or_31cf_blocked_reactivation
no_go_conditions.trigger: any of the 7 31CH legacy-sidecar exclusion globs match a key in the manifest OR any of the 9 31CH runtime safety invariants is false (ffn_down_residual_enabled, no_activation_capture_artifact_in_runtime_path, no_build_ffn_patch, no_g_prt_sidecar_root_set_write, no_legacy_prt_sidecar_entries)
no_go_conditions.action: refuse to start, escalate to operator
no_go_conditions.why: a match indicates the dormant Phase 11BB / 24P / 28BR-AT sidecar machinery is being reactivated; this is the EXACT scenario that caused the 31CF BLOCKED classification; an operator must review before any consumer is allowed to proceed

no_go_conditions.name: real_sha256_placeholders
no_go_conditions.trigger: any of the 15 q2k_sha256 or sdir_sha256 placeholders is not equal to the string 'metadata_only_placeholder'
no_go_conditions.action: refuse to start, escalate to operator
no_go_conditions.why: a manifest that claims a real SHA-256 for a file is asserting that a real binary was written; 31CO is metadata-only and does NOT write binaries; a real SHA-256 in a metadata-only manifest is a lie; an operator must review whether the manifest is correctly classified

no_go_conditions.name: provenance_rules_filtered_or_silently_bypassed
no_go_conditions.action: refuse to start, escalate to operator
no_go_conditions.why: the 31CN adapter pattern requires silent_bypass_used == false and r_provenance_filtered == []; if the 7 R-provenance rules were filtered or bypassed, the derivative-extension adapter pattern was not used, which means the manifest is either a 31CL-style filtered manifest (deprecated) or a corrupted derivative; an operator must review

artifact_hygiene_assertions.note: the 31CO planning doc and JSON are pure markdown + JSON; no Python source, no compiled binary, no model file, no Q2_K/SDIR blob, no raw activation. The 31CI validator's 31CH-derived hardcoded-operator-path patterns (/media/matthew-villnave, /home/matthew-villnave, VL_usb, /root/) are USED in the planning doc only as rejection-pattern examples (per the sdi_artifact_hygiene_scan false-positive rule), not as live paths.

claim_boundary.this_is: metadata-only planned manifest consumer / loader preflight planner (planning-only design phase; no loader implementation)
claim_boundary.this_is_not: a real artifact writer, a runtime loader, a runtime integration, or a runtime-loadable binary

allowed_claims: A metadata-only planned manifest consumer / loader preflight planner was designed.
The 31CO planning doc defines a future consumer contract (8-step preflight, 6 loader-readiness checks, 12 fail-fast rules, 4 no-go conditions) without implementing the consumer.
The 31CO planning doc and JSON are metadata-only; no Python source, no compiled binary, no model file, no Q2_K/SDIR blob, no raw activation is created.

The 31CO preflight inherits the 31CI validator's 50 rules in 8 categories, the 31CM round-trip validator's 12 checks, the 31CN derivative-extension adapter pattern, and the 31CH 9-runtime-safety-invariants + 7-legacy-sidecar-exclusion-globs.
The 31CO loader-readiness boundary evaluates 6 conditions; all 6 must PASS for the consumer to emit PASS_LOADER_READINESS.
The 31CO fail-fast rules include 12 rules, 5 of which (R_LF_08 through R_LF_12) are also no-go conditions that escalate to operator review.

The 31CO no-go conditions (NGC_01 through NGC_04) defend against (1) non-metadata-only manifests, (2) legacy-sidecar / 31CF BLOCKED reactivation, (3) real-SHA-256 in placeholders, and (4) silent provenance bypass.
The 31CO planning doc does NOT prove future runtime viability, does NOT enable the legacy PRT/SDI sidecar, and does NOT implement a loader.
forbidden_claims: no actual Q2_K / SDIR artifacts were generated
no claim that 31CO proves future runtime viability
no claim that the consumer is a deployable loader
no 'real activations behave like synthetic Gaussian' claim
no claim that 31CO is a runtime integration
no claim that 31CO enables live runtime memory savings
claim_boundary_check_method: the 31CO planning doc and JSON use only the 10 standard + 5 31CO-specific cautious phrases from sdi-claim-boundary-scan catalog; the 18 forbidden claims (per the sdi-claim-boundary-scan 15-family + 3 31CO-specific families) are NOT used; the planning doc and JSON have been reviewed against the sdi-claim-boundary-scan forbidden-claim catalog and pass all 18 families (no matches found).

next_allowed_phase_recommendation.next_phase: Phase 31CP — Metadata-Only Planned Manifest Consumer / Loader Preflight Implementation (proposed; NOT in scope for 31CO; only with explicit operator approval)
next_allowed_phase_recommendation.rationale: 31CO is a planning-only design phase. The consumer contract, preflight sequence, loader-readiness boundary, fail-fast rules, and no-go conditions are documented. A future 31CP would implement the consumer in Python (src/phase31cp_planned_manifest_consumer.py), calling the 31CI validator at step 5 and the 31CM round-trip validator at step 6. 31CP would be a metadata-only implementation phase, only with explicit operator approval, and only if the operator chooses to advance.
alternative_next_phases: 31CP-R (Loader Preflight Implementation Repair, only if 31CP is rejected post-merge or incomplete)
31CG (Larger Prompt/Token Sensitivity Planning, planning-only, only if explicitly requested)
31CR (Loader Pre-Flight Actual Loader Smoke Test, only after 31CP passes, only with operator approval)

wall_clock_estimate: <1 min on this host (planning + JSON generation, Python stdlib only)
